human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for funeral, cemetery, crematory, vital-record, family-authority, consent, survivor-benefit handoff, communication, exception, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to funeral-family service and support resolution, evidence-backed communication, sensitive-record handling, repeat-failure escalation, and adjacent-owner handoff rather than generic customer care.

Authority sources cover FTC Funeral Rule regulation and guidance, CDC/NCHS death-registration workflow, California funeral and cemetery regulator guidance, VA burial benefit guidance, SSA survivor benefit guidance, and OSHA funeral-home safety interpretation.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, missing safe-resolution details, policy exceptions, unsupported workarounds, adjacent grief/estate/benefit requests, repeat failure, terminology normalization, disputed family authority, veterans cemetery handoff, privacy conflict, missing tenant systems, and audit-ready resolution.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/funeral-cemetery-and-end-of-life-services/family-care-specialist/ and remains distinct from arrangement conference, funeral compliance, licensed funeral director or embalmer, cemetery administration, crematory operations, documentation, preneed, billing, benefits, legal, privacy, and grief-support lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated family care specialist for death-care family support, communication, sensitive evidence handling, repeat-failure escalation, and adjacent-owner routing.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
